game_name: Dune II: The Building of a Dynasty

danger_signals.visual_form: Marauding gigantic sandworm entity on map
danger_signals.meaning: Uncontrollable environmental threat capable of destroying vehicles and infantry; blocks player access to certain areas but deterred by rocky terrain
Active enemy presence and military capability; indicates threat level and enemy technological advancement

danger_signals.visual_form: Bumps on terrain surface in spice fields
danger_signals.meaning: Hidden danger—unit destruction if triggered by shooting or collision; requires careful harvesting strategy
Unknown enemy positions and environmental hazards; areas not yet scouted by player units

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual degradation of buildings over time without concrete foundations
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental harsh conditions; lack of maintenance indicates vulnerable or abandoned positions

safety_signals.meaning: Safe building zone; blocks sandworm movement; primary construction area for player base
Reduced structure decay; indicates established, defended positions; long-term settlement

safety_signals.visual_form: Orange coloration on sand (darker orange = higher concentration)
safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability and economic safety; controls player expansion strategy and base placement

safety_signals.visual_form: Units within player's view radius actively displayed
safety_signals.meaning: Explored and secured territory; removes fog of war permanently in explored areas

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: House sigil visible in mission briefings and structure designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Atreides units and buildings visually distinct from Harkonnen and Ordos counterparts
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Base structures reflect Atreides aesthetic; Sonic Tank as signature unit; unique technology tree progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: House sigil visible in mission briefings and structure designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Heavy armor units (Devastator tanks, Quad light vehicles); aesthetically brutal architecture; cannot build trikes
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Emphasis on heavy armor and ordnance in unit design; base structures appear more militaristic and imposing

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: House sigil visible in mission briefings and structure designs
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Specialized nerve gas units (Deviator tank); faster raider trikes instead of standard trikes; unique technological approach
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tech-focused architecture; unit designs emphasize speed and unconventional warfare (mind control via nerve gas)

faction_indicators.color_identity: Imperial coloring (distinct from primary three houses)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Elite heavy infantry units; appear only in endgame as temporary alliance with remaining enemy houses
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Signals endgame state and political shift; represents imperial authority and final challenge

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Large fog of war coverage; sparse enemy presence; unoccupied territories with visible spice fields; single player base
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Harvesters actively collecting spice; minimal combat; units focus on exploration and base construction

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Reduced fog of war; visible enemy bases in adjacent territories; multiple faction-colored structures on map; spice field competition visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased military unit production; defensive perimeter construction; harvester protection becomes priority

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Majority of map explored; two enemy factions' bases consolidated in remaining territories; clear visual territorial division by faction color
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Full military mobilization; aggressive offensive operations; harvester withdrawal from contested zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sardaukar elite units appear on map; two remaining enemy houses' armies visibly combined; player's single base under siege conditions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All resources directed to final defensive/offensive push; maximum unit production; no harvesting operations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: New building types unlock visually; advanced unit designs replace basic units; faction-specific super weapons become available
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Unit composition shifts toward higher-tier units; tactical options expand; mission difficulty increases with opponent capability

environmental_storytelling.technique: Resource visibility as narrative—spice field coloration
environmental_storytelling.example: Orange sand coloration intensity communicates wealth distribution; darker orange signals richer territories, driving player territorial conquest narrative
Each house's base structures visually reflect their culture—Atreides minimalist/refined, Harkonnen brutal/industrial, Ordos tech-focused—communicates political philosophy without dialogue
Unpredictable, uncontrollable sandworm roaming map communicates Arrakis as hostile primordial force; players must respect natural boundaries (rocky terrain) to survive
Buildings gradually deteriorate without maintenance, communicating the harsh Arrakis environment and economic costs of territorial holding

environmental_storytelling.technique: Fog of war as political obscurity
environmental_storytelling.example: Permanently removed fog once explored signals concrete knowledge acquisition; unexplored areas represent political uncertainty and risk
Harkonnen heavy armor units vs. Ordos speed/control units vs. Atreides balanced approach visually communicates each house's strategic doctrine

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves to spice fields, collects resources, returns to refinery; indicates economic activity and base security
Halts harvesting; attempts to flee or relies on military escort; signals economic vulnerability and threat escalation
Move slowly, spread across map removing fog of war; no aggressive posturing
Formations tighten; movement becomes defensive; units position for engagement; signals shift to combat readiness
Continuous new unit construction visible; indicates economic strength and escalating threat level
Reduced unit production; passive defenses only; structure deterioration visible; signals faction weakness and victory proximity
Appear suddenly on map; aggressive movement toward player; signals political shift from competition to imperial intervention

notes: Dune II pioneered the RTS template by establishing visual language for resource management (spice field coloration), faction identity (house colors and unit aesthetics), environmental hazards (sandworm and decay mechanics), and world state progression (fog of war removal, territory control, endgame alliance shift). The game communicates political complexity through gameplay structure: initial three-way competition visually represented by separated base colors; mid-game alliance mechanics shown through combined enemy forces; endgame imperial intervention shown via Sardaukar unit appearance. Environmental storytelling dominates—no dialogue required to understand each house's philosophy through unit design, structure aesthetics, and tactical capabilities.
sources_used: wikipedia—Dune II gameplay mechanics, faction descriptions, unit types, world state structure, environmental mechanics
